title: Pilosocereus sublanatus
taxonomy: Pilosocereus sublanatus is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosocereus, which is a group of columnar cacti native to the Americas. Pilosocereus sublanatus is further classified as a member of the subgenus Pilocereus, which is characterized by its tall stems and dense spination. The species is often associated with other columnar cacti, such as Pilosocereus azureus and Pilosocereus chrysacanthus. Pilosocereus sublanatus is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Pilosocereus sublanatus, commonly known as the 'Tree Cactus', is a species of cactus native to South America, characterized by its tall, columnar stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and skin infections, due to its reported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
